name: William Ruto
title: President

education.title: Bachelor of Science in Botany and Zoology
education.description: Undergraduate science degree in botany and zoology, completed in 1990. He worked his way through university from a modest Kamagut upbringing and was a leader in Christian student groups, where his organizing talent first surfaced before politics.

education.title: Master of Science in Plant Ecology
education.description: Returned to the University of Nairobi mid-career for graduate research in plant ecology, completing the degree in 2011 while a senior national politician and reviving the scientific track he had set aside for two decades.

education.title: Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Plant Ecology
education.description: Doctoral research on how human activity shapes plant diversity in the Saiwa swamp ecosystem of western Kenya, completed in 2018, making him the first sitting Deputy President to earn a PhD from a Kenyan public university.

professional.title: Assistant Minister for Home Affairs
professional.description: First executive post after capturing the Eldoret North seat in 1997. Under President Moi he handled internal administration and security matters, learning government from the inside while still in his early thirties.

professional.title: Minister for Agriculture
professional.description: Ran the agriculture docket in the grand-coalition cabinet formed after the 2007 election crisis, overseeing fertilizer subsidy programmes and the maize sector during a severe food-price squeeze before a 2010 reshuffle.

professional.title: Minister for Higher Education, Science and Technology
professional.description: Held the higher education, science and technology portfolio during the coalition government's final stretch, supervising university expansion before leaving to build the United Republican Party for the 2013 race.

professional.title: Deputy President of Kenya
professional.description: Kenya's first Deputy President under the 2010 Constitution, elected on a joint ticket with Uhuru Kenyatta in 2013 and re-elected in 2017. The pair's later falling-out set up the underdog campaign that won him the presidency in 2022.

contacts.title: Office
contacts.value: Office of the President, Harambee Avenue

bio: William Ruto is Kenya's fifth President, a chicken-seller-turned-plant-ecologist who rode a hustler movement from the Kamagut roadside to State House in one of Africa's great political climbs. Elected Eldoret North MP in 1997, he mastered the inside game under Moi, ran the agriculture and higher education dockets in the grand coalition, then became the 2010 Constitution's first Deputy President on a joint ticket with Uhuru Kenyatta. When the handshake left him isolated, he recast himself as the outsider of bottom-up economics and won the 2022 election against the establishment that made him. In office he has pushed the housing levy, a universal health rollout and aggressive tax reform, drawing credit for boldness and street protests over the cost of living in equal measure. A University of Nairobi PhD in plant ecology, he remains the most relentless campaigner of his generation.

name: Martha Karua
title: President
bio: Martha Karua is the iron lady of Kenyan politics: magistrate turned second-liberation lawyer, reforming justice minister and party leader now making her boldest bid yet for the presidency. She defended political prisoners when doing so was dangerous, held the Gichugu seat for two decades, and steered the justice docket through the 2005 referendum and the National Accord talks before resigning on principle over stalled reforms. The 2022 cycle made her Azimio's running mate and the first woman on a leading Kenyan presidential ticket. Uncompromising on the rule of law and graft, she has built her 2027 bid around constitutionalism, women's leadership and restoring an accountable state.

education.title: Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.)
education.description: Read law at the University of Nairobi from 1977 to 1980. Her legal training carried her through a magistracy and into the human rights litigation of the 1990s that made her one of the second-liberation movement's leading lawyers.

education.title: Master of Business Administration (MBA)
education.description: Mid-career business administration degree from USIU-Africa in Nairobi, taken to add management depth to a public career spent in courtrooms, cabinet and party leadership rather than in business or the academy.

education.title: Diploma in Law (Advocates Training Programme)
education.description: Completed the advocates training programme at the Kenya School of Law and was admitted to the roll of advocates in 1981, the professional gateway to her long courtroom career and later stewardship of the justice ministry.

professional.title: Advocate
professional.description: Served as a district magistrate through most of the 1980s, then opened her own practice in 1987, taking on political and human rights briefs, including landmark election petitions, that put her at the heart of the multiparty struggle.

professional.title: Minister for Justice, National Cohesion and Constitutional Affairs
professional.description: Ran the justice, national cohesion and constitutional affairs docket through the 2005 constitutional referendum and the 2008 crisis talks that produced the National Accord, resigning in 2009 over stalled judicial reforms.

professional.title: Party Leader
professional.description: Leads the People's Liberation Party, the renamed NARC-Kenya she has fronted since 2008. She was Azimio's deputy-presidential running mate in 2022 and now carries the party's banner into the 2027 presidential race.

contacts.title: Office
contacts.value: Nairobi Liberation House, Woodlands Road, off Lenana Road.

name: Kalonzo Musyoka
title: President
bio: Kalonzo Musyoka is Kenya's patient man of diplomacy, a former Vice President and long-serving foreign minister whose Wiper machine has made him the perennial bridge-builder of opposition politics. A lawyer who entered parliament at 32 through a Kitui North by-election, he chaired regional peace talks on Somalia and Sudan and earned the mediator's reputation he has traded on ever since. He broke with Kibaki over the 2005 referendum, co-founded the Orange movement, then served as Vice President through the grand-coalition years. Several times a presidential-ticket principal or deputy, he now leads the Wiper Patriotic Front into 2027 arguing that his steadiness is the antidote to Kenya's turbulence.

education.title: Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree
education.description: Studied law at the University of Nairobi after his schooling in present-day Kitui County, graduating in 1977. The degree launched a career that moved quickly from legal practice into elective politics and diplomacy.

education.title: Postgraduate Diploma in Law (Advocates Training Programme)
education.description: Completed the advocates training programme at the Kenya School of Law in 1978 and was admitted to the bar, practising only briefly before winning the Kitui North parliamentary seat in a 1985 by-election.

professional.title: Minister for Foreign Affairs
professional.description: Kenya's top diplomat under President Moi, chairing regional peace initiatives on Somalia and Sudan and building the mediator's reputation that has anchored his public image through every campaign since.

professional.title: Minister for Foreign Affairs (second tenure)
professional.description: Returned to the foreign affairs docket in President Kibaki's first cabinet, representing the new NARC government abroad during the optimism of the post-KANU transition before moving to the environment ministry.

professional.title: Minister for the Environment and Natural Resources
professional.description: Ran the environment and natural resources ministry in the Kibaki government, a short tenure that ended when he broke with the administration over the 2005 constitutional referendum and joined the Orange rebellion.

professional.title: Vice President of Kenya
professional.description: Appointed Vice President after the disputed 2007 election, serving through the grand-coalition years and the 2010 constitutional transition as President Kibaki's principal assistant and the government's senior diplomat.

contacts.title: Office
contacts.value: Sunrise Court on Kufuga Road, off Langata Road in the Karen neighborhood of Nairobi, Kenya.

name: Fred Matiang'i
title: President
bio: Fred Matiang'i is the technocrat's technocrat: the literature don turned Jubilee enforcer-in-chief who cleaned up national exams and ran the interior ministry with unapologetic firmness. He came to cabinet from lecture halls, publishing houses and governance programmes rather than campaign rallies, and it showed: digital TV migration delivered over industry protest, exam cheating crushed in a single cycle, and a security docket run so assertively that colleagues dubbed him the 'super CS'. After a spell abroad following the 2022 transition, he returned to mount a presidential bid promising competence without drama: a state that simply works. His doctorate in comparative literature makes him the rare contender happier citing delivery reports than slogans.

education.title: Bachelor's Degree in Education
education.description: Trained as a teacher at Kenyatta University and taught English and literature before moving into university lecturing, publishing and governance consulting, the technocratic route through which he eventually reached the cabinet.

education.title: Master's Degree in English
education.description: Graduate study in English at the University of Nairobi, deepening the language and communication specialism he taught at Kenyan universities and applied in education-sector and publishing work across East Africa.

education.title: PhD in Communication and Comparative Literature
education.description: Doctorate in communication and comparative literature from the University of Nairobi. Before joining government he lectured, served with university regulators and ran governance programmes for international development organisations.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for ICT
professional.description: First cabinet post in the Jubilee administration, where he drove Kenya's digital television migration against fierce industry resistance and pushed early e-government platforms, marking him out as a forceful implementer.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Education
professional.description: Took over the education docket and crushed industrial-scale cheating in national examinations within a single cycle, overhauling exam administration and KNEC governance in a crackdown that made him a household name.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Interior and Coordination of National Government
professional.description: Ran the interior ministry for five years, coordinating security operations, national administration and the Huduma Namba registration drive, and chaired cabinet delivery committees that earned him the informal 'super CS' tag.

contacts.title: Office
contacts.value: Kileleshwa area of Nairobi, off Mandera Road.

name: Boniface Mwangi
title: President
bio: Boniface Mwangi is Kenya's loudest conscience: the award-winning photojournalist who put down his camera to fight impunity in the streets and now wants to carry that fight into the presidency itself. His unflinching images of the 2007-08 post-election violence twice won him the CNN Multichoice Africa Photojournalist of the Year award and changed how Kenya saw itself. He founded PAWA254 to weaponize creativity for civic causes, led protests that cost him arrests and beatings, and ran for Starehe MP in 2017 on a crowd-funded Ukweli Party ticket. Schooled by hardship before NYU's documentary programme polished the craft, he frames his presidential bid as the ordinary hustler's revolt against a captured state.

education.title: Diploma in Bible Studies
education.description: Theological study at a Nairobi bible college in his youth, taken during years of hardship in which he sold books on the street; he credits the period with the discipline that later carried him into journalism training.

education.title: Diploma in Print Journalism
education.description: Journalism training at the East African School of Journalism that took him straight into newsroom photography, where his images of Kenyan politics were soon running on front pages and international wire services.

education.title: Human Rights and Documentary Photography (non-degree program)
education.description: Selected for a human rights and documentary photography programme at New York University after his post-election violence archive drew global attention; the fellowship shaped the activist turn his work took back home.

education.title: Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education (KCSE), private candidate
education.description: Sat the national secondary examination as an adult private candidate, completing formal schooling that poverty had interrupted in his teens and publicly making the point that education can be reclaimed at any age.

professional.title: Photojournalist
professional.description: Staff photographer whose unflinching images of the 2007-08 post-election violence became defining records of the crisis and twice won him the CNN Multichoice Africa Photojournalist of the Year award before he left news for activism.

professional.title: Founder
professional.description: Civic movement he founded to organize young Kenyans around accountability, documentation of police brutality and voter mobilization, turning his street-protest activism into a structured national outfit.

professional.title: Parliamentary Candidate, Starehe Constituency (Ukweli Party)
professional.description: Contested Nairobi's Starehe parliamentary seat in 2017 on the Ukweli Party ticket, running a crowd-funded grassroots campaign that finished third yet marked his shift from protest activism to electoral politics.

professional.title: Founder, PAWA254
professional.description: Founded PAWA254, the Nairobi creative-activism hub where photographers, artists and organizers collaborate on civic campaigns; it incubated protest art and documentary projects across the 2013 and 2017 election cycles.

name: David Maraga
title: President
bio: David Maraga is the judge who said no: the former Chief Justice who led Africa's first nullification of a sitting president's re-election and now asks voters to send that rectitude to State House. Admitted to the bar in 1978, he built a Nakuru practice over a quarter-century, rose through the High Court and Court of Appeal, and captained the Supreme Court through the historic 2017 annulment. A devout Seventh-day Adventist who never sat on Saturdays, he defended judicial independence against budget cuts and open defiance until his 2021 retirement. His campaign rests on a simple promise: the constitution, enforced without fear or favour.

education.title: Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
education.description: Law degree from the University of Nairobi followed by the advocates course at the Kenya School of Law; he was admitted to the bar in 1978, the start of a legal career that ran from provincial practice to the country's top judicial office.

professional.title: Advocate
professional.description: Ran Maraga & Company Advocates in Nakuru for a quarter-century, specializing in civil and commercial litigation and rising through the Law Society ranks before his elevation to the High Court bench in 2003.

professional.title: Judge of the High Court
professional.description: Appointed a High Court judge in 2003, serving in Mombasa and Nakuru. A devout Seventh-day Adventist who declined to sit on Saturdays, he built a reputation for strict case management and personal incorruptibility.

professional.title: Judge, Court of Appeal
professional.description: Elevated to the Court of Appeal in 2012, presiding over its Kisumu bench and chairing the judiciary's committee on election preparations, the assignment that made him the courts' electoral-law specialist.

professional.title: Chief Justice & President of the Supreme Court
professional.description: Kenya's Chief Justice and President of the Supreme Court from 2016 to 2021. He led the bench that nullified the 2017 presidential election, a first in Africa, and defended judicial independence against sustained executive pressure.

name: Abdullswamad Sherrif Nassir
title: Governor
bio: Abdullswamad Sherrif Nassir is a Kenyan politician serving as the second Governor of Mombasa County. Born in Majengo, Mombasa, he previously represented Mvita Constituency as Member of Parliament. Affiliated with the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), he emphasizes coastal economic development, enhanced security, and preservation of cultural heritage.

name: Fatuma Achani
title: Governor
bio: Fatuma Achani is a Kenyan politician who made history as the first female governor in the Coast region, winning the Kwale gubernatorial election as a member of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party.

name: Issa Juma Boy
title: Senator
bio: Senator for Kwale County.

name: Gideon Mung'aro
title: Governor
bio: Gideon Mung'aro is a Kenyan politician who has served as Governor of Kilifi County since August 2022. He previously represented Kilifi North Constituency in the National Assembly following the 2013 election, and is a former Mayor of Malindi, having also represented Malindi Constituency in parliament before it was split.

name: Stewart Madzayo
title: Senator
bio: Stewart Madzayo is a Kenyan politician born in Chonyi, Bahari Constituency, Kilifi County. He is the Senator for Kilifi County and a member of the Orange Democratic Movement.

name: Danson Mungatana
title: Senator
bio: Danson Mungatana is a Kenyan lawyer and politician serving as Senator for Tana River County, born on 9 August 1970. He has served in various roles, including Member of Parliament for Garsen Constituency.

name: Abass Sheikh Mohammed
title: Senator
bio: Abass Sheikh Mohamed is a Kenyan politician serving as Senator for Wajir County. He previously served in the 11th Parliament of Kenya and has held various roles, including CEO of the Kenya Livestock Marketing Council.

education.title: Diploma in Range Management and Degree in Natural Resource Management
Post-graduate Diploma in Sustainable Local Economic Development and MBA in Strategic Marketing

professional.title: Member of the 11th Parliament of Kenya

name: Mohamed Adan Khalif
title: Governor
bio: Mohamed Adan Khalif is the governor-elect of Mandera County, having previously served as speaker of the Mandera County Assembly. He won the election with 53,185 votes.

name: Mohamed Said Chute
title: Senator
bio: Chute Mohamed Said is a Kenyan politician serving as Senator for Marsabit County, elected in the August 2022 general election under the UDA banner. He chairs the Standing Committee on National Cohesion, Equal Opportunity and Regional Integration.

name: Kawira Mwangaza
title: Governor
bio: Kawira Mwangaza served as Meru County's third Governor from 2022 until March 2025, when the High Court upheld her third impeachment by the county assembly, ending a turbulent tenure. A bishop and broadcaster who rose from selling bananas to owning Baite TV, she won the seat as an independent after a term as Meru Woman Representative, but her administration was consumed by a running war with MCAs over appointments and conduct. She was the first Kenyan governor removed through impeachment while serving her first term.

name: Isaac Mutuma M'ethingia
title: Governor
bio: Isaac Mutuma M'ethingia is Meru County's fourth Governor, sworn in on 17 March 2025 after the High Court upheld the impeachment of Kawira Mwangaza, whom he had served as Deputy Governor. A reverend and businessman, he assumed office under Article 182 of the Constitution and inherited a county government strained by years of conflict between the executive and the assembly, making reconciliation and service delivery the twin tests of his tenure.

name: Kathuri Murungi
title: Senator
bio: Kathuri Murungi is a Kenyan politician and Member of Parliament for South Imenti Constituency in Meru County. He has served on several parliamentary departmental committees and declared his interest in the Senatorial seat ahead of the 2022 general election.

education.title: Bachelor of Science in Natural Resources Management

name: Gataya Mwenda
title: Senator
bio: Gataya Mwenda is the current Senator for Tharaka Nithi County, elected in August 2022 on the United Democratic Alliance ticket. He served as Member of the County Assembly for Tharaka Nithi from 2013 to 2022, with a professional background in the civil service from 1990 to 2007.

name: Cecily Mbarire
title: Governor
bio: Cecily Mutitu Mbarire is the Governor of Embu County and Chairperson of the UDA Party, having served in various roles including Member of Parliament and Deputy Minister for Tourism and Transport.

education.title: Global Executive Masters in Business Administration
Bachelors of Arts in Economics and Sociology
Bachelor of Arts in Economics and Sociology
Post-graduate Diploma in Gender and Development Studies

professional.title: Deputy Minister for Tourism and Transport

name: Alexander Mundigi
title: Senator
bio: Alexander Mundigi Munyi is the Senator for Embu County, elected in 2022 on a Democratic Party ticket, having defied the odds to win the seat against candidates from more dominant political parties.

education.title: Bachelor’s degree in biblical studies
education.institution: Covenant Theology Seminary in North Carolina

name: Enock Kiio Wambua
title: Senator
bio: Enoch Wambua Kiio is a Kenyan politician and Senator for Kitui County, born on 31 October 1972, with a background in journalism and education from Moi University and other institutions.

education.title: Bachelor of Education in English Literature
Diploma in Planning and Management of Development Studies

name: Agnes Kavindu
title: Senator
bio: Agnes Kavindu is a Kenyan politician and the current Senator for Machakos County. Born in Ngonda Village, Matungulu, Machakos County, in the early 1960s, she began her education at Ngonda Primary School before joining a local secondary school in Machakos.

professional.title: Member of the County Public Investments & Special Funds Committee

name: Moses Badilisha Kiarie
title: Governor
bio: Dr. Moses Ndirangu Kiarie Badilisha is the Governor of Nyandarua County, elected in August 2022 on a United Democratic Alliance ticket. Born in Munyeki village, he has a background in engineering and energy development.

name: John Methu
title: Senator
bio: John Methu Muhia is a Kenyan politician and the current Senator of Nyandarua County. Born on 14 February 1991 in Kinangop Constituency and raised by his grandmother, he pursued a bachelor's degree in Political Science from Pwani University and developed a strong interest in governance, leadership, and public policy.

professional.title: Personal Secretary to the Speaker of the Nyandarua County Assembly

name: James Murango
title: Senator
bio: James Kamau Murango is the Senator for Kirinyaga County, elected in 2022, and chairs the Senate's Agriculture, Livestock and Fisheries Committee.

name: Irungu Kang'ata
title: Governor
bio: Irungu Kang'ata is the Governor of Murang'a County, born in 1980 in Murang'a town. He previously served as Murang'a County Senator and Majority Chief Whip for the Jubilee party in the Kenya Senate.

professional.title: Majority Chief Whip
professional.institution: Jubilee party in the Kenya Senate

name: Joe Nyutu
title: Senator
bio: Joe Nyutu is a Kenyan politician and the current Senator for Murang'a County under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party. Born in 1965 in Murang'a County, he has a background in education.

name: Kimani Wamatangi
title: Governor
bio: Paul Kimani Wamatangi is a Kenyan politician serving as Governor of Kiambu County.

name: Karungo wa Thang'wa
title: Senator
bio: Paul Karungo Wa Thang'wa is a Kenyan politician serving as Senator of Kiambu County. Born into a family of thirteen siblings, he began his education at Mitahato Primary School before graduating from the University of Nairobi with a bachelor's degree in development communication. His political career began in 2013 when he was elected MCA for Ngewa Ward.

education.title: Diploma in Journalism and Radio production

professional.title: News editor, reporter, continuity presenter, and standby presenter

name: Jeremiah Lomorukai
title: Governor
bio: Jeremiah Ekamais Lomorukai is the Governor of Turkana County, elected in 2022, having previously served two terms as Member of Parliament for Loima Constituency.

name: Julius Murgor
title: Senator
bio: Recha Julius Murgor is a Kenyan politician and the current Senator for West Pokot County. Born into the Pokot community, he founded the Pokot Outreach Ministry with his wife Rachel, entered formal politics in 2007, and has since focused on development, peace-building, and social welfare initiatives.

education.title: Master of Arts in Religion and Philosophy

professional.title: Chair
professional.institution: Kenyan Senate Committee on Labour and Social Welfare

name: Jonathan Lati Lelelit
title: Governor
bio: Jonathan Lelelit Lati is the Governor of Samburu County, elected in 2022. He previously served two terms as Member of Parliament for Samburu West Constituency, from 2013 to 2022.

education.title: Bachelor of Science in Mathematics and Economics

professional.title: Chairman
professional.institution: Kenya Industrial Research and Development Institute (KIRDI)

name: George Natembeya
title: Governor
bio: George Natembeya is the Governor of Trans Nzoia County, having assumed office in October 2022. With a background in public service, he has also expressed interest in a future presidential bid.

name: Allan Chesang
title: Senator
bio: Allan Kiprotich Chesang is a Kenyan businessman and politician serving as the current Senator of Trans Nzoia County.

professional.title: CEO
professional.institution: Transcentury Global and Uplift Group of companies

name: Jonathan Bii
title: Governor
bio: Jonathan Bii is the Governor of Uasin Gishu County and a member of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party, elected in 2022. He has a background in education and community development.

education.title: Master's degree in project planning and management

name: Jackson Mandago
title: Senator
bio: Jackson Kiplagat Arap Mandago is a Kenyan politician who has served as Governor of Uasin Gishu and is currently the county's Senator. Born and raised in Uasin Gishu, he was educated at Kenyatta University and the Catholic University of Eastern Africa.

education.title: Master's degree in Procurement and Strategic Planning

name: Wisley Rotich
title: Governor
bio: Wisley Rotich is the second Governor of Elgeyo Marakwet County, having previously served as Deputy Governor from 2017 to 2022. He holds a master's and bachelor's degree in Economics from Moi University and has worked at the Kenya Wildlife Service and Equity Bank.

education.title: Masters and Bachelors Degree in Economics

name: John Kinyua
title: Senator
bio: John Kinyua Nderitu is a Kenyan politician serving as Senator for Laikipia County, with a background in pharmaceuticals and business. He has been actively involved in politics since 2007.

education.title: Bachelor of Science in Chemistry & Botany

name: Patrick Ole Ntutu
title: Governor
bio: Patrick Keturet Ole Ntutu is the Governor of Narok County, elected in 2022, following a long career in public service.

name: Erick Mutai
title: Governor
bio: Erick Kipkoech Mutai is a Kenyan academic and politician serving as Governor of Kericho County. Born on 28 December 1983, he worked as a university lecturer before entering politics.

education.title: Master's degree in Literature and Communication

name: Aaron Cheruiyot
title: Senator
bio: Aaron Cheruiyot is a Kenyan politician born on 23 February 1986 in Kapchebet, Kabianga. Elected Senator for Kericho County, he served as Majority Leader of the Senate from 2022. He is married to Linah Chesang Cheruiyot and has one child.

name: Hillary Sigei
title: Senator
bio: Hillary Kiprotich Sigei is a senatorial aspirant in Bomet County.

name: Fernandez Barasa
title: Governor
bio: Fernandes Barasa is the Governor of Kakamega, having won the gubernatorial seat in the 2022 election. A member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), he has a background in accounting and corporate leadership.

education.title: Bachelor of Commerce degree in Accounting

professional.title: Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director

name: Boni Khalwale
title: Senator
bio: Boni Khalwale is a Kenyan politician and the current Senator of Kakamega, elected on a UDA party ticket, known as a chief critic of the Ruto administration.

name: Godfrey Osotsi
title: Senator
bio: Godfrey Atieno Osotsi is a Kenyan politician serving as Senator for Vihiga County, elected in August 2022. He previously served as a nominated Member of the National Assembly of Kenya from 2017 to 2022.

education.title: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
Executive MBA
education.institution: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT)

professional.title: Chairperson of the County Public Investments and Special Funds Committee

name: Kenneth Lusaka
party: Forum for Restoration of Democracy- Kenya
title: Governor

name: James Orengo
title: Governor
bio: James Aggrey Bob Orengo is a Kenyan lawyer, human rights activist, and politician who is the current Governor of Siaya County. Born in 1951 in Kasipul Kabondo, Homa Bay County, he has held several government positions, including Senator of Siaya County and Minister for Lands.

professional.title: Minority Leader of the Senate of Kenya

name: Tom Ojienda
title: Senator
bio: Tom Ojienda is a Senior Counsel and politician from Kisumu who won the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) Senate ticket for Kisumu.

name: Gladys Wanga
title: Governor
bio: Gladys Atieno Wanga is a Kenyan politician and the current Governor of Homa Bay County. A member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), she served as Member of Parliament for Homa Bay Woman Representative from 2017 to 2022.

name: Eddy Oketch
title: Senator
bio: Eddy Oketch Gicheru is a Kenyan politician and Senator for Migori County, born on 12 August 1991 and elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. He has a background in education and social entrepreneurship and has led initiatives promoting youth empowerment and environmental sustainability.

education.title: Diploma in Leadership, Entrepreneurship, African Studies, and Community Service Projects

name: Simba Arati
title: Governor
bio: Paul Simba Arati is a Kenyan politician and the current Governor of Kisii County, elected in 2022. A member of the Orange Democratic Movement, he previously served as Member of Parliament for Dagoretti North Constituency.

education.title: Master of Business Administration (MBA) in Strategic Management
Master's degree in International Relations
education.institution: Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology

name: Erick Okong'o Omogeni
title: Senator
bio: Erick Okong'o Omogeni is a Kenyan politician who served as Nyamira County Senator from 2017 to 2022. Born in 1970 in Nyamira division, he holds a Bachelor of Law degree from the University of Nairobi.

education.title: Masters Degree in Governance and Leadership
education.institution: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology

name: Johnson Sakaja
title: Governor
bio: Johnson Sakaja is the current Governor of Nairobi City County, born on 2 February 1985. He previously served as Nairobi Senator and a nominated MP, and is a member of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party.

professional.title: National Chairman of The National Alliance (TNA)

name: Edwin Sifuna
title: Senator
description: Won the Nairobi Senate seat in 2022 on an ODM ticket, five years after his first attempt, and made it the county's loudest oversight perch: grilling city budgets and carrying his party's floor fights until his 2026 break with ODM.
bio: Edwin Sifuna is Nairobi's Senator and the sharpest tongue in Kenya's opposition: the lawyer who ran Raila Odinga's ODM machine for eight years before it cast him out for refusing to soften on Ruto. An advocate since 2008, he cut his teeth on CORD and NASA strategy committees before Raila handed him the ODM Secretary-General's desk in 2018, at just 35, and he became the party's enforcer, spokesman and most quotable defender. Elected Nairobi Senator in 2022, five years after losing the seat to Johnson Sakaja, he built a national following on combative debate-floor performances. When ODM embraced cooperation with President Ruto's government, Sifuna refused to fall in line: the party stripped him of the Secretary-Generalship in 2026 and the Senate minority dewhipped him on 16 July. He answered with Linda Mwananchi, a street movement rallying cost-of-living anger alongside Babu Owino and James Orengo, positioned as a third force ahead of 2027.

education.title: Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education
education.description: Schooled at Kakamega Township Primary and Musingu High School in western Kenya, where he sat his KCSE; those Kakamega roots still anchor his politics, with Luhya leaders now fronting him as a national standard-bearer.

education.title: Bachelor of Laws
education.description: Read law at the University of Nairobi, graduating in 2006. The training shows in his political style: floor debates argued like courtroom submissions and party disputes fought clause by clause through tribunals and registrars.

education.title: Postgraduate Diploma in Law
education.description: Completed the advocates training programme at the Kenya School of Law in 2007, did his pupilage at Kairu & McCourt Advocates in Nairobi, and was admitted to the Roll of Advocates in 2008, the profession he practised before politics swallowed his calendar.

professional.title: Partner
professional.description: Founding partner of his own Nairobi law practice after admission to the bar in 2008, building the commercial and political litigation experience that later made him ODM's in-house legal brain through petitions and coalition talks.

professional.title: Technical/Advisory Committee Member
professional.description: Backroom strategist on the technical and advisory committees of the CORD and later NASA opposition coalitions, drafting positions and coalition agreements in the Raila Odinga camp's engine room across two election cycles.

professional.title: Secretary-General
professional.description: Ran Kenya's biggest opposition party from February 2018 as Raila Odinga's enforcer and its public voice, until the NEC removed him in 2026, a removal the Registrar of Political Parties upheld that July, for opposing ODM's cooperation pact with the Ruto administration.

professional.title: Deputy Minority Whip
professional.description: Held the Senate minority's deputy whip post from 2022 until 16 July 2026, when the minority party voted him out days after his ODM ouster and replaced him with Senator Eddy Oketch; he thanked colleagues and congratulated his successor.

professional.title: Convener
professional.description: Fronts the Linda Mwananchi ('protect the citizen') movement with Babu Owino and James Orengo, rallying crowds against the cost of living and taxation and building what allies bill as a third force, possibly his own presidential vehicle, for 2027.

name: Cyril Bill Mwoni
title: MP
bio: A skilled youth leader with a servant-leadership mindset who puts people's interests first. Having served as a student leader on campus and worked with various community-based NGOs, he has gained the skills for impactful leadership.

name: Kevin Otieno
title: MP
bio: Otieno Japeng' stands for a 50% reduction in secondary day school fees, equitable distribution of resources such as bursaries, economic transformation, free medication for senior citizens, and proper, inclusive leadership representation.

name: Ephraim Omondi
title: MP
bio: Let's make Embakasi East great: let's work together.

name: Irungu Maina
title: MP
bio: Douglas Irungu Maina, a son of Ndia, was shaped by the voices of a new generation during the 2024-2025 Gen Z demonstrations, which he describes as a call for justice, accountability, and a future for young people. As an MP aspirant for Ndia in 2027 under the Jubilee Party, he pledges to champion youth empowerment, economic opportunities, and leadership that truly listens.

name: Joel K Ngetich
title: MP
bio: Committed to change by bringing cohesion to and developing Kipkelion East.

name: Paul Nyongesa
title: MP
bio: Paul Nyongesa is a trainee healthcare professional from the University of Nairobi and a former student leader. He founded the Sirisia Tunavyoitaka Movement, serves as elected chairman of the Bungoma West Alumni CBO (community-based organization), and is a youth leader in the Kabila La Vijana Movement at both constituency and county level. His vision for Sirisia Constituency is rooted in three key SDGs (Sustainable Development Goals): no poverty, quality education, and good health.

name: Dennis Achimbo
title: MP
bio: A servant leader committed to integrity, opportunity, and progress, raised within the community he seeks to serve. His mission is to amplify constituents' voices in the Senate, champion accountable leadership, and push for practical solutions that create jobs and empower youth and women, building a future rooted in transparency, fairness, and shared prosperity.

name: David Asunda
title: MP
bio: Born in Kasarani Constituency, he began his primary education at Precious Hope Preparatory, where he excelled, before joining Paul Boit High School in Uasin Gishu. He later pursued tertiary education at St. Francis School of Nursing, and describes himself as hardworking and ambitious.

name: Jimi Richard Wanjigi
title: President
bio: Jimi Wanjigi is the kingmaker who wants the crown: the billionaire dealmaker behind three Kenyan presidencies who has stepped out of the back rooms to lead the Safina Party's own bid for State House. Heir to a political-business dynasty and chief executive of the Kwacha Group family office, he bankrolled Kibaki's 2002 NARC triumph, brokered the Uhuru-Ruto Jubilee ticket of 2013, then masterminded Raila Odinga's 2017 NASA campaign, a defection that brought police sieges to his Muthaiga home. His 2022 presidential bid collapsed over degree requirements; he answered by finishing the degree and doubling down. Pitching himself as the insider who knows where the money hides, he fronts Safina's 2027 campaign on jobs, debt sanity and ending state predation.

education.title: High School Certificate
education.description: Completed secondary school at St. Mary's School Nairobi, the elite Catholic school whose alumni include Uhuru Kenyatta; the friendships and networks formed there foreshadowed the political circles he later moved in.

education.title: Business Studies
education.description: Left for Toronto in 1982 to study business at York University. Accounts differ on whether he finished the degree before returning to Kenya to build businesses alongside his father, former cabinet minister Maina Wanjigi.

education.title: Bachelor's Degree
education.description: Undergraduate degree completed at Daystar University decades into his business career; the timing of his graduation became a contested point in his 2022 presidential clearance dispute with the electoral commission.

education.title: Master's in Diplomacy, Development and International Studies
education.description: Postgraduate study in diplomacy, development and international studies at Daystar University, with a heavy security-studies component, pursued while mounting his first presidential campaign ahead of the 2022 election.

professional.title: Co-Founder & Director
professional.description: Co-founded one of Kenya's first private garbage collection and waste management companies soon after returning from Canada, an early-mover venture that introduced him to Nairobi's procurement world and seeded later businesses.

professional.title: Director
professional.description: Directs a private financial services and international cash transfer venture handling cross-border commercial transactions, one strand of the discreet web of companies through which he manages his family's interests.

professional.title: Chief Political Strategist & Financier
professional.description: Worked the back rooms of the 2002 NARC campaign as mobilizer, strategist and financier, helping end KANU's four-decade rule and earning the kingmaker reputation that followed him through two more election cycles.

professional.title: Lead Broker & Campaign Financier
professional.description: Brokered and bankrolled the 2013 alliance between Uhuru Kenyatta and William Ruto, hosting the negotiations that fused their parties into the Jubilee ticket which captured the presidency in the March 2013 election.

professional.title: Chief Campaign Strategist
professional.description: Crossed to the opposition as chief strategist and financier of Raila Odinga's 2017 NASA campaign, a switch that made him a state target and saw police twice lay siege to his Muthaiga home during the post-election standoff.

professional.title: Chief Executive Officer
professional.description: Runs the Wanjigi family office, a holding group spanning agribusiness, real estate, financial services and industrials, and the commercial platform from which he has financed successive national political campaigns.
